what: Cross-host p99 variance: identical code (one HEAD build) run on N freshly-provisioned Hetzner CCX23 instances, one canonical capture each, to characterize between-host benchmark variance.
distribution_shape: bimodal — a fast cluster near 5ms and a slow cluster near 13.3ms, with no hosts between
cause_status: VERIFIED via clock probe: reported cpu_model / NUMA / governor are identical across both clusters, but actual /proc/cpuinfo 'cpu MHz' differs (slow-tail hosts ran at 2400 MHz, the faster-tail host at 2197 MHz) -- the CCX23 'dedicated vCPU' SKU is served on physically heterogeneous hosts. Note: lower clock correlated with BETTER tail, so it is host-config/silicon heterogeneity, not a simple clock-speed effect.

probe.method: per-box /proc/cpuinfo cpu MHz + lscpu + stepping alongside p99
probe.lesson: Cloud 'dedicated vCPU' is not hardware-homogeneous. The spec sheet (model, vCPU, RAM) was identical across a 2.5x p99 tail difference; only a direct clock probe distinguished the hosts. Measure on multiple freshly-provisioned instances.
